title: Fiery Ethiopian Fish Flatbread
description: Spicy, peppery Ethiopian fish flatbread that mimics injera's airy texture and showcases crisp white fish fillets in a zesty sauce.

ingredients.name: clarified butter (niter kibbeh) or olive oil

instructions: Mix chickpea flour, water, salt and yeast in a bowl until smooth, cover and let sit 30 minutes for batter to rise.

Heat a nonstick skillet over medium heat, pour a ladle of batter, swirl to form a thin circle, and cook for 2 minutes until bubbles form, then flip and cook 1 minute more; repeat to make 4 flatbreads.
Season fish fillets with lemon juice, smoked paprika, and a pinch of salt, then set aside.
In the same skillet melt clarified butter, add sliced onion and garlic, sauté until golden.
Stir in berbere spice mix, cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add seasoned fish fillets to the skillet, cook 3-4 minutes on each side until flaky and cooked through.
Reduce heat and pour remaining batter into the skillet, swirling to coat the fish and create an airy, pizza‑like crust.
Let the mixture cook for 4 minutes, then flip carefully, cooking another 3 minutes until the batter is crisp and the fish is evenly coated.
Remove from heat, sprinkle chopped cilantro over the top, and let rest 2 minutes.
Slice into wedges, serve hot, and enjoy the smoky, peppery flavor of Ethiopian-inspired fish flatbread.
